name: Sauteed Prawns with Fried Leeks in Prawn-Carr
recipeIngredient: deveined (reserve the heads and shells for the Prawn-Carrot Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the prawns with salt and pepper.
2. Refrigerate the prawns for 1 hour.
3. In a large skillet, heat olive oil on medium-high until hot. Add chilled prawns and sauté them for 2 minutes on each side, or until done. Set aside and keep warm.
4. In a large saucepan, heat peanut oil on medium until hot (350°F). Add leeks and fry for 1 minute, or until golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on paper towels to drain. Season lightly with salt.
5. In each of 4 plates, place 1/2 cup Prawn-Carrot Sauce. Arrange 4 prawns in a circular pattern on top of the sauce. Place fried leeks on top.
6. To make Prawn-Carrot Sauce: Heat oil on medium-high in a saucepan. Add prawn shells and heads, sauté for 4 minutes. Remove and set aside. Add onions, carrots, and leeks; sauté for 5 minutes. Return shells and heads to the pan. Add white wine; cook until reduced by 1/4 cup. Add heavy cream; cook until reduced by 1/2 cup. Discard shells and heads. Blend sauce in a food processor, strain, and return to pan. Whisk in butter pieces one at a time, season with salt and pepper.
